title: Full-Depth Storage Daybed (Captain's Bed)
summary: A twin daybed built from three joined bench modules, each with an optional drawer — full-depth under-bed storage rather than the shallow drawers found in typical captain's beds.
description:
This daybed is really three separate bench modules joined side by side, each one deep enough front-to-back to hold a full-depth drawer — a genuinely different storage capacity than a typical captain's bed, whose drawers usually only reach partway under the mattress. Build each module as its own box first: sides, a center support, and a bottom, so each section is a complete, square unit before anything gets joined to its neighbors.

**The three modules attach to each other along their shared sides**, so it matters that each one is built square and to the same height, or the daybed will rock or the top rail won't sit flat across all three. Add the back rail and side rails once the modules are joined, tying the whole assembly into a single rigid frame rather than three boxes that happen to be touching.

The drawers are optional per module — build all three, some, or none, depending on how much of the storage you actually want accessible versus how much can just be enclosed. Drawer fronts get attached last with an even reveal gap, and the whole piece takes a standard twin mattress once the frame is complete.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut six module sides at 74" and three module center supports at 22" from the 3/4" plywood, full sheets. Cut three module bottoms at 74" from the 3/4" plywood, full sheets. Cut one top rail at 75", one back rail at 75" and two side rails at 22" from the 2x2. Cut six drawer sides at 20" and six drawer front/back at 20-1/2" from the 1x4. Cut three drawer bottoms at 20" from the 1/4" plywood, full sheet. Cut three drawer faces at 23" from the 1x3. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to three 8 ft 2x2, three 8 ft 1x4 and one 8 ft 1x3. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the three modules
steps.body: Assemble each module's sides, center support, and bottom into a square box using 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue. Build all three modules identically, checking each is square before moving to the next.

steps.title: Join the modules
steps.body: Set the three modules side by side and join them along their shared sides with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws, checking they sit flush and level with each other.

steps.title: Add the rails
steps.body: Attach the top rail, back rail, and side rails across the joined modules to tie the whole assembly into one rigid frame.

steps.title: Build the drawers (optional)
steps.body: For any module you want a drawer in, build the drawer box with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, attaching the bottom with brad nails and glue. Install on drawer slides.

steps.title: Attach drawer faces and finish
steps.body: Attach the drawer faces with an even reveal gap, add pulls, sand, and finish the entire piece.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Twin daybed built from three joined storage modules
